species: Denitrifying/fermentative enrichment community (Wadden Sea tidal-flat sediment; dominant denitrifiers = Rhodobacteraceae)

growth_records.medium: Defined anoxic marine medium (glucose+acetate+7 amino acids, nitrite/nitrate)

up.method: steady-state: D x (influent-effluent) = 0.36/day x (20 - <1 mM) ~ 0.36 x 19 = 6.84 mM/day
D x influent = 0.36/day x 1 mM = 0.36 mM/day (nitrate ~completely consumed)
D x influent = 0.36/day x 28 C-mM = 10.08 C-mM/day

sec.method: complete denitrification to N2 (Rhodobacteraceae); general statement ~1-5% of nitrate to N2O; no absolute rate tabulated

growth_records.cite: Anna Hanke et al. Selective Pressure of Temperature on Competition and Cross-Feeding within Denitrifying and Fermentative Microbial Communities. Frontiers in Microbiology (2016). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2015.01461. [PMC4703780]
growth_records.method: calculated: dilution rate 0.36 volume changes/day = 0.36/24 = 0.015 h-1; at chemostat steady state mu=D=0.015 h-1; td=ln2/0.015=46.2 h
growth_records.snippet: the concentrations in the influent medium were maintained at 20 mM for nitrite, 1 mM for nitrate and 28 mM carbon for the 25 C cultures... in 2011 and in 2012, denitrification was stable at 25 C... the dilution rate was gradually increased
growth_records.notes: MIXED sediment enrichment community, NOT a single strain (organism-level rates cannot be assigned). Chemostat 2.8 L, anoxic, pH 8.0, 25 C, stable complete denitrification (Rhodobacteraceae dominant). mu=D=0.36 vol/day=0.015 h-1 at steady state; td=46.2 h. Volumetric consumption rates derived as D x (influent-effluent): nitrite 6.84 mM/day, nitrate 0.36 mM/day, carbon 10.08 C-mM/day. Nitrite kept <1 mM in vessel. No per-gDW biomass rates available.
